central_claims: AM-μP generalizes μP to heterogeneous multi-path architectures
Optimal LR scales as L^(-3/2) universally across CNNs, ResNets, Transformers
Zero-shot cross-depth LR transfer is enabled by the power law
strengths: First unified depth-scaling analysis across CNNs, ResNets, Transformers
Principled AM-μP formalization with merge-consistency axioms
Extensive empirical confirmation on CIFAR-10/100, ImageNet (3-8% CIFAR deviation)
Extends μP to depth dimension, filling a genuine gap

issues_found.description: Zero-shot cross-depth transfer — the headline practical claim — is never validated end-to-end. Experiments show α ≈ -1.5 but never demonstrate that the predicted LR at depth L2 (from LR at depth L1) actually achieves good accuracy.
ViT-ImageNet exponent is -1.178, a 22% deviation from theoretical -1.5. Most practically important configuration. Unexplained.
Grid search uses epoch-1 training loss as optimal LR proxy. Not validated against final accuracy or convergence. Disconnects from practical use case.
Theory assumes clean initialization without normalization, but BatchNorm raises exponent to -1.701 vs -1.435. Normalized networks are the dominant practice.

reasoning.novelty_assessment: Genuine novelty: AM-μP and depth-transfer unify prior μP work across architectures. Builds on Jelassi et al. (2023) for MLPs, extends to multi-path networks. Bordelon & Pehlevan (2025) is concurrent and partially overlapping on residual scaling.
reasoning.decision_implication: Theory and derivations are solid. Practical validation has a central gap (no transfer demonstration). ICML-borderline: likely weak accept if transfer experiments are added, borderline if not.
